## PAGE 01192: Using drag and drop

- page_id: `191928529`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928529/Using+drag+and+drop
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.545+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['drag', 'drop', 'select-drag-drop-rule', 'drag-and-drop']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create relationships or assign property values by dragging and dropping one element on to another right in the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There are a number of predefined rules to create relationships or assign property values automatically. You may also set up your own drag-and-drop specifications between any elements.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element onto another, hold it for a few seconds and then release it. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element and drag it onto another (This action is valid only for Windows operating system users). A shortcut menu is displayed.
2. From the shortcut menu, select one of the rules you want to apply.

The following table lists the predefined drag-and-drop rules. These rules define the possible results when dragging the source element on the target element.

| Source Element | Target Element | Created Relationship | Assigned Property |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Activity | Capability | Maps To Capability |  |
| Actual Condition | Activity |  | Activity Performable Under Condition |
| Actual Condition | Actual Effect |  | Enabling Condition |
| Actual Condition | Actual Resource |  | Actual Condition |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928532 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01193: Using libraries

- page_id: `191928532`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928532/Using+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-11-06T14:35:45.168+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['libraries', 'universal-joint-task-list', 'ujtl', 'dod-it-standards-registry', 'disr', 'joint-conditions-library', 'add-standard-policy-from-disr-library']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

715818604

715818606

715818603

**On this page**

715818605

UAF plugin supports three element libraries:

1. The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).
2. DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).
3. Joint Conditions.

The **Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)**, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UAF UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.

The **DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)** is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UAF, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UAF element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.

The **Joint Conditions Library** supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.

##### Library versions

You can choose which UJTL or DISR library version to use for your project. By default, libraries will be loaded, but the versions will not be set (except for the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool, where the latest library versions will be set).

There are the following ways of working with library versions:

- start a new project and work with the existing version of the libraries.
- start a new project with Cameo Enterprise Architecture and choose different version of the libraries (you can choose one of the existing or download using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD190'] ).
- open the existing project with libraries that are in the current modeling tool's version.

###### Choosing library version

When creating a new project, you can choose which library version you will be using.

- By default, the libraries version is not selected for NAF, NAF4, MODAF, UAF EA, and UAF projects.
- By default, the latest libraries version is selected for the DoDAF projects.

To choose which library version will be used

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a project' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
2. In the New Project dialog, General options, choose the needed version of the preferred library (see the figure below).
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing library version

You can change the library version at any time.

To change library version

1. In the Containment tree toolbar, click Options and choose Show Auxiliary Resources . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tree_toolbar.png']
2. In the Containment tree, right-click the library which version you want to change.
3. From the library shortcut menu, choose Change Library Version and then click the needed version. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_library_version.png']

If the selected library version does not contain elements of the previously used library version, such elements will be marked DELETED, but still remain in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

**Case Study**

Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.

To add a standard/policy from the DISR library

1. Create the StdV-1 diagram.
2. Create new or add an existing system element.
3. In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the Edit button. The Select Standard dialog opens.
4. Click the DISR Library tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.
5. Select the standard and do one of the following:
  - Double-click the selected standard to add it to the Selected elements list.
  - Click the + button to add the selected standard to the Selected elements list.
6. Click OK , after you finish selecting the standards.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928538 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01194: Using SysML elements in UAF plugin

- page_id: `191928538`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928538/Using+SysML+elements+in+UAF+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T11:15:32.732+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['expand-sysml-toolbars', 'use-sysml-elements-in-updm-2-plugin']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML elements can be used to extend UAF provided set of concepts. SysML toolbars in UAF diagrams are collapsed by default.

For better understanding how to use SysML elements in UAF plugin models two case studies are described:

##### Case Study 1. The Requirement usage in OV-2 diagram****

We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.

To use the Requirement in an OV-2 diagram

1. Create an OV-2 diagram.
2. Create an Operational Performer in the diagram pane.
3. Expand the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar and select Requirement.
4. Create it in the diagram pane.
5. Create the Satisfy relationship between the Operational Performer and the Requirement.

As you can see in the following figure, the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Case Study 2. Describing a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model

The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML elements in the UAF model.

The case study uses the sample project Counting FTEs (requires CST).mdzip, which can be found in <MagicDraw installation folder>\samples\UAF.

Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model.

First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='UAF12P2024xR2']). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.

Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SysML elements can be used to extend UAF provided set of concepts. SysML toolbars in UAF diagrams are collapsed by default.</p><p>For better understanding how to use SysML elements in UAF plugin models two case studies are described:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42ca2c21-25d1-4a19-96ee-d57bf29dcfd5" /></p><h3>Case Study 1. The Requirement usage in OV-2 diagram<strong><br /></strong></h3><p>We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.</p><p>To use the Requirement in an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create an OV-2 diagram.</li><li>Create an Operational Performer in the diagram pane.</li><li>Expand the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar and select Requirement.</li><li>Create it in the diagram pane.</li><li>Create the Satisfy relationship between the Operational Performer and the Requirement.</li></ol><p>As you can see in the following figure, the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_SysML.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Case Study 2. Describing a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model</h3><p>The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML elements in the UAF model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41d943c4-9265-4d20-af6c-51837017cc52"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The case study uses the sample project Counting FTEs (requires CST).mdzip, which can be found in &lt;MagicDraw installation folder&gt;\samples\UAF.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model.</p><p>First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_typical_CST.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instantiating structures" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.</p><p>Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual_CST.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parametric_diagram_in_UPDM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="581e272e-c0da-4542-a0ad-7f84f5214c6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929283 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01196: Using To Do property

- page_id: `191929283`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929283/Using+To+Do+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-12T10:19:32.946+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked "added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.

To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property

1. From the main toolbar click Edit > Find TODO . All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the Search Results tab.

For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State *Searching (Aircraft)* before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability *Search* (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked &quot;added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main toolbar click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.<br />All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4a2d810-8712-4983-8a2e-5d42a26d7ad6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State <em>Searching (Aircraft)</em> before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability <em>Search</em> (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_capability_search.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using To Do property" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01198: Value Item

- page_id: `191929159`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929159/Value+Item

- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:24:48.033+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Data Type

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item.

The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind:

- Benefit
- Cost
- KPI
- Loss
- Other
- Quality
- Revenue
- Time

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Enumeration

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929161 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01200: Value Stream

- page_id: `191929161`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929161/Value+Stream
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:24:31.101+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Instance Specification</p>
````

A property of a Whole Life Configuration, used in version control of a Versioned Element. It asserts that a Versioned Element is a version of a Whole Life Configuration.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929253 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01205: View mapping

- page_id: `191929253`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929253/View+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T12:12:52.348+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Svcv-6 | SV-6 | The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Svcv-6 Role-based | SV-6 Role-based | The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Service Channels Summary Table | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |
| Service Channels Summary Matrix | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and <span>UAF 1</span>.1.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.1</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Svcv-6</td><td>SV-6</td><td>The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Svcv-6 Role-based</td><td>SV-6 Role-based</td><td>The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Table</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Matrix</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929270 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01206: View Mappings

- page_id: `191929270`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929270/View+Mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-20T10:25:22.781+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 1.x and UPDM 2.0.

| UPDM 1.x | UPDM 2.0 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CV-3 | CV-2 | CV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the CV-3 Capability Phasing chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| CV-5 | CV-2 | CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-3 | StV-2 | StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the StV-3 Capability Taxonomy chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-5 | StV-2 | StV-5 Organization Deployment Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The StV-5 tabular form of representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| PV-2 | PV-1 | PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram. Instead of the diagram, the PV-2 Project Timelines chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| AcV-2 | AcV-1 | AcV-2 Programme Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram. Instead of the diagram, the AcV-2 Acquisition Clusters chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SV-8 | SV-1 | SV-8 Capability Configuration Management diagram is refactored to meet UPDM 2.0 requirements. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification diagram. New form of the SV-8 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SvcV-1 | SOV-1 |  |
| SvcV-2 | SOV-2 |  |
| SvcV-3a | Service Channels Summary Matrix | Service Channels Summary Matrix is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis and creation. |
| SvcV-3b | SV-12 |  |
| SvcV-4a | SOV-5 structural |  |
| SvcV-4b | SOV-5 behavioral |  |
| SvcV-5 | Dependency Matrix |  |
| SvcV-6 | Service Channels Summary Table | Service Channels Summary Table is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. |
| SvcV-7 | Generic Table |  |
| SvcV-10a | SOV-4a |  |
| SvcV-10b | SOV-4b |  |
| SvcV-10c | SOV-4c |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 1.x and UPDM 2.0.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 81.0811%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.6444%;" /><col style="width: 14.2436%;" /><col style="width: 77.112%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 1.x </th><th>UPDM 2.0 </th><th><p>Comments</p></th></tr><tr><td>CV-3 </td><td>CV-2 </td><td><p>CV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the CV-3 Capability Phasing chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td>CV-5 </td><td>CV-2 </td><td><p>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td>StV-3 </td><td>StV-2 </td><td><p>StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the StV-3 Capability Taxonomy chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">StV-5 </td><td colspan="1">StV-2 </td><td colspan="1"><p>StV-5 Organization Deployment Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The StV-5 tabular form of representation is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">PV-2 </td><td colspan="1">PV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram. Instead of the diagram, the PV-2 Project Timelines chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">AcV-2 </td><td colspan="1">AcV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>AcV-2 Programme Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram. Instead of the diagram, the AcV-2 Acquisition Clusters chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SV-8 </td><td colspan="1">SV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SV-8 Capability Configuration Management diagram is refactored to meet UPDM 2.0 requirements. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification diagram. New form of the SV-8 diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-1</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-2 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-2</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-3a </td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Matrix</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Matrix is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis and creation.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-3b </td><td colspan="1">SV-12</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-4a </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-5 structural</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-4b </td><td colspan="1">SOV-5 behavioral</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-5 </td><td colspan="1">Dependency Matrix</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-6</td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Table</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Table is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis, creation, and modification.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-7 </td><td colspan="1"><p>Generic Table</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>SvcV-10a</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-4a</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>SvcV-10b </span></td><td colspan="1"><span>SOV-4b</span></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>SvcV-10c</span></td><td colspan="1"><span> SOV-4c</span></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 01207: Viewpoint

- page_id: `191929166`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929166/Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:23:35.004+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An architecture viewpoint frames (to formulate or construct in a particular style or language) one or more concerns. A concern can be framed by more than one viewpoint.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A concern can be framed by more than one viewpoint. </p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Class</p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929169 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01210: Whole Life Configuration Kind

- page_id: `191929169`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929169/Whole+Life+Configuration+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:23:02.882+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Whole Life Configuration. Its enumeration literals are:

- Service - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which Services are versioned.
- ResourcePerformer - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which ResourcePerformers are versioned.
- Organizational Resource - Indicates that the Whole Life Configuration associated with the Whole Life Configuration Kind is the master specification from which Organizational Resources are versioned.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Whole Life Configuration. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Service - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which Services are versioned.</li><li>ResourcePerformer - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which ResourcePerformers are versioned.</li><li>Organizational Resource - Indicates that the Whole Life Configuration associated with the Whole Life Configuration Kind is the master specification from which Organizational Resources are versioned.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01211: Whole Life Enterprise

- page_id: `191929170`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929170/Whole+Life+Enterprise
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:22:50.759+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Whole Life Enterprise is a purposeful endeavor of any size involving people, organizations and supporting systems. It is made up of Temporal Parts and Structural Parts.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

It is made up of Temporal Parts and Structural Parts. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927359 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01212: Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy

- page_id: `191927359`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927359/Working+with+A8+Standards+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.548+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Standards Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the A8 and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > A8 Standards Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for A8 Standards Taxonomy, and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Standard element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Standard (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_create_standard.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the A8 package, and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Standard (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Standard (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Standard (or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Generalization.
3. Connect the Standard elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If needed, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927368 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01213: Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `191927368`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927368/Working+with+A8+Standards+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-23T14:12:40.044+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Standards Taxonomy Table (A8)**provides all the available technical and non-technical standards, guidances, and policies.

To add a new Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard in the Standards Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.

To add an existing standard in the Standards Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Standard dialog, choose the desired Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Standards Taxonomy Table (A8)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available technical and non-technical standards, guidances, and policies.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard in the Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing standard in the Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Standard </strong>dialog, choose the desired Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927999 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01214: Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table

- page_id: `191927999`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927999/Working+with+Actual+Project+Milestone+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-09T09:04:36.531+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The** **Actual** **Project** **Milestone Summary** **Table (Pj-Rm)** depicts all actual project milestones in the model.

Adding the actual project milestones into the table, you can see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table.

To create a table

1. In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new table.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - On the main toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the Create Diagram button.
3. In the opened window, search for Actual Project Milestone Summary Table . An empty table is created.

When you have a table, you can start filling it out. Here you can add new or already existing in the model elements.

To add new Actual Project Milestone

1. In the table, click the Add New button. The new Actual Projet Milestone is created.
2. Fill out the needed columns.

To add existing model elements

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the table toolbar, click **Add Existing**. In the open **Select Actual Project Milestone**dialog, choose which elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
  - In the Containment tree, select Actual Project Milestone and drag it to the table.
2. Click **OK** when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The</strong> <strong>Actual</strong> <strong>Project</strong> <strong>Milestone Summary</strong> <strong>Table (Pj-Rm)</strong> depicts all actual project milestones in the model.</p><p>Adding the actual project milestones into the table, you can see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Create Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span> .</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened window, search for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="297c4c37-d037-447b-813d-084eb89828b1"> Actual Project Milestone Summary Table</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br />An empty table is created.</li></ol><p>When you have a table, you can start filling it out. Here you can add new or already existing in the model elements.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3cdcbc4d-8f66-463e-b4d7-372b6d650fc7">To add new Actual Project Milestone</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new Actual Projet Milestone is created.</li><li>Fill out the needed columns.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add existing model elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. In the open <strong>Select Actual Project Milestone </strong>dialog, choose which elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af0cc9ad-aae0-4c41-9090-ab112a1b858b"><ac:rich-text-body>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the Containment tree, select Actual Project Milestone and drag it to the table.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928034 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01215: Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `191928034`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928034/Working+with+Actual+Projects+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-23T08:37:01.207+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282638040

1282638053

1282638043

**Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1282638039

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4534a74b-7d1d-459d-8539-0857b4ec5dec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282638039</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with matrices" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928089 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01216: Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix

- page_id: `191928089`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928089/Working+with+Actual+Resource+Taxonomy+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-23T08:43:12.293+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources > Actual Resources Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283014949

1283014961

1283014951

The **Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix** (Ar-Tx) describes the mapping between the actual responsible resources and the actual responsibilities.

The Rows of this matrix are Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Post) and the Columns are Actual Responsibility.

To create an Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Actual Responsible Resources);
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Actual Responsibility);
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Responsible Resources map to Actual Responsibility through the Responsible For relationship.

To map the Actual Responsible Resource to Actual Responsibility

- Double-click the empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relationship

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1283014948

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928083 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01217: Working with Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191928083`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928083/Working+with+Actual+Resources+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-07T08:56:35.651+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources > Actual Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Resources Connectivity diagram is used to model typical and actual forms of the organizational relationships.

To create an typical Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship. You can display:the Competenceof the Organizational Resources.the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Actual Resources Connectivity diagram is used to model typical and actual forms of the organizational relationships.</p><p>To create an typical Actual Resources Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Organizations, </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual Actual Resources Connectivity diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Persons</span>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Relate Actual Organizational Resources with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Organization Relationship</span></span>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Resources Structure diagram is used to model typical and actual forms of the organizational relationships.

To create an typical Actual Resources Structure diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual Actual Resources Structure diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship. You can display:the Competenceof the Organizational Resources.the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928037 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01219: Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `191928037`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928037/Working+with+Actual+Responsible+Resources+to+Actual+Projects+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-24T12:54:20.167+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282670460

1282670463

1282670462

The Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, andActual Posts) for Projects.

The rows in the Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix represent Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To create an Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Responsible Resources map to Actual Projects using the Responsible For relationship.

##### Mapping an Actual Responsible Resource to an Actual Project

To map an Actual Responsible Resource to an Actual Project

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282670459

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927436 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01220: Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

- page_id: `191927436`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927436/Working+with+Actual+Strategic+Phases+Gantt+Chart
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.006+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

The Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart shows how Actual Strategic Phases change over time. The rows represent the Actual Strategic Phase (Enterprise Mission,Actual Enduring Task,Whole Life Enterprise,Actual Enterprise Phase,Value Stream) in the roadmap. The bars represent the related information of the Actual Strategic Phase.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR2'] chapter.

##### Creating Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

When you choose to create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** opens.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart from the wizard

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Strategic Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_actual_enterprise_phases_gantt_chart.png']
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart** check box.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** is disabled

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Strategic Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
2. Type the chart name. (Optional)
3. Click Finish .

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Creation Wizard property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Strategic Phase

To add an Actual Strategic Phase

1. On the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New and choose one of the following:
  - Add New Enterprise Mission
  - Add New Actual Enduring Task
  - Add New Whole Life Enterprise
  - Add New Actual Enterprise Phase
  - Add New Value Stream
2. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**
3. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Actual Strategic Phase

1. On the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**
3. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Adding Sub Actual Strategic Phase

To add a new Sub Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Gantt chart tree, select the Actual Strategic Phase for which you want to create a Sub Actual Strategic Phase.
2. In the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Strategic Phase, click Add New Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Gantt chart tree, select the Actual Strategic Phase for which you want to add an existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase.
2. In the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Strategic Phase, click Add Existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

When you choose to add new or existing Actual Strategic Phases, they will be listed for selection depending on the defined structure.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Displaying all suppressed or removed from the chart

When an Actual Strategic Phase is displayed in *italic*, it means that some sub elements were removed from the chart. You can restore them back with the click of one button.

To display all sub elements removed from the chart

1. Select the Actual Strategic Phase which sub elements you want to restore.
2. In the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

The **Display All** button also expands the suppressed structure.

##### Deleting elements and sub elements

You can either remove the element or sub element from the chart or delete it from the model.

To remove an element or sub elementfrom the chart

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete an element or sub element from the model

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Hiding or displaying columns

You can choose to hide or display the Actual Strategic Phase Name, Start Date, or End Date columns.

To show/hide the columns

1. In the diagram toolbar, click Show Columns.
2. In the menu, select the needed columns.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024xR2']

## PAGE 01221: Working with Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927474`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927474/Working+with+Actual+Strategic+Phases+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.229+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283102180

1283102192

1283102182

**The Actual Strategic Phases to** **Capabilities** **Mapping Matrix (St-Tr)** describes the mapping between the required capabilities and the actual strategic phases that those actual strategic phases support directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

The Rows of this matrix are Actual Strategic Phases and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Strategic Phases);
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Actual Strategic Phases map to Capabilities using Phases relationship.

To map an Actual Strategic Phase to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1283102179

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
