<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926777 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01222: Working with AcV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926777`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926777/Working+with+AcV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:10:18.249+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413746516

413746518

413746514

**On this page**

3

413746517

##### Creating AcV-2 diagram

When you choose to create AcV-2 diagram, AcV-2 Creation Wizard opens by default.

To create an AcV-2 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click AcV-2 Project Timeliness .
3. In the AcV-2 Project Timeliness dialog, click Add . AcV-2 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click Next .
5. Add the project(s). Click Next .
6. Set the chart properties.
7. Click **Finish**. If you do not wish to use the AcV-2 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create AcV-2”.Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show PV-2 / AcV-2 / NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to False.

To create an AcV-2 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click AcV-2 Project Timeliness .
3. In the AcV-2 Project Timeliness dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.
5. Click OK . The blank AcV-2 diagram is created.

You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.

##### Adding actual projects to AcV-2 diagram

You can add a new or an existing actual project to the AcV-2 diagram.

To add a new actual project

1. On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.
2. Specify a time frame for the actual project.

To add a new actual sub project

1. Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.

To add an existing actual project

1. On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Deleting actual projects from AcV-2 diagram

To delete an actual project from the chart

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete From Chart**. Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.

To delete an actual project from the model

1. Select an actual project. 
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete**.

##### Relating actual projects in AcV-2 diagram

You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.

You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate actual projects

1. Select two actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between actual projects

1. Select two related actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None.

To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Available validation suggestions

If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.

##### Adding Milestones in AcV-2 diagram

To add a milestone

1. Select an actual project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kind.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
5. Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click Finish when you are done.

##### Deleting milestones in AcV-2 diagram

To delete a milestone

1. Select a milestone.
2. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Delete .

##### Applying actual project status in AcV-2 diagram

To apply the actual project status to a new milestone

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the actual project status by following steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

To edit the actual project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

Specify Project Status

##### Removing actual project status in AcV-2 diagram

To remove the project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , click the Remove project status indicator mark button.
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68ad7570-5a2f-4d4b-8a61-f2c67fe1ddc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413746516</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2f82754-09d3-4ac2-8732-ada794fee7e2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413746518</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fa04b1e-18d5-4a05-b14e-4b9baa4ef295"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413746514</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f3908bd-78b3-4476-bf81-736789e7b061"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="578ef716-1a5e-4322-8a22-d6c10d73bec7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413746517</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create AcV-2 diagram, AcV-2 Creation Wizard opens by default.</p><p>To create an AcV-2 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>AcV-2 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Add the project(s). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Set the chart properties.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a0015f-2f05-4453-ba31-37f223a88dc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the AcV-2 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create AcV-2”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UAF</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show PV-2 / AcV-2 / NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to False.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To create an AcV-2 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li>Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank AcV-2 diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c07458a6-ac7a-43e3-814c-ac3f25c28eaf"><ac:rich-text-body>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Adding actual projects to AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>You can add a new or an existing actual project to the AcV-2 diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt;<strong> Actual Project</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual sub project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click <strong>Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Deleting actual projects from AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To delete an actual project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete From Chart</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccf057ea-e981-493b-9cb5-9ade4d266358"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete an actual project from the model</p><hr /><p>1. Select an actual project.<br />2. On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p><h3>Relating actual projects in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p><br /></p><p>To relate actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation between actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select None.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the suggested solutions.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relate_in_acv2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with AcV-2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Available validation suggestions</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78e284ce-2000-4dbc-8a6a-9bee4430c047"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Adding Milestones in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To add a milestone</p><hr /><p><br /></p><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kind.</li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Deleting milestones in AcV-2 diagram </h3><p>To delete a milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a milestone.</li><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Applying actual project status in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To apply the actual project status to a new milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Apply Status</strong>.</li><li>Specify the actual project status by following steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the actual project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53d41aa7-6355-4f27-a5aa-33b7aa796a17"><ac:rich-text-body>If two or more milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Specify Project Status</strong> and then select the milestone you want to edit the status for.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing actual project status in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To remove the project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>, click the Remove project status indicator mark button.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927385 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01223: Working with Architecture Management diagram

- page_id: `191927385`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927385/Working+with+Architecture+Management+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.674+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Architecture Management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Architecture Management diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select any of the Architecture Management view specification packages (except Architecture Management Information) and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Architecture Management . [ATTACHMENT filename='architecture_diagram_new.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Architecture Management and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service Architecture element, but the same is valid for other available elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_viewpoint_element.png']
2. Name the element.

Creating elements from the Containment tree differs from creating them in the diagram. The element availability depends on which Architecture Management view specification package you choose. In this example, the Service Architecture element creation from the Architecture Management Traceability package in the Containment tree is described, but any other element from a different package can be created in the same way.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Architecture Management Traceability package, and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_service_architecture.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Architecture(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Service Architecture will be connected to the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Implements relationship.

To connect the elements with the Implements relationship

1. Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Implements.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927080 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01224: Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `191927080`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927080/Working+with+C1+Capability+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-23T14:43:10.889+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)** provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)</strong> provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 01225: Working with conjugated Interfaces

- page_id: `191928543`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928543/Working+with+conjugated+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-09T14:56:36.131+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The <viewpoint name>*Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization ofInterface that has the same features as its original Interface except that itsFlow Propertiesare reversed (conjugated).

To create a Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface

- Right-click one or more interfaces and select Tools > Create Conjugated <viewpoint name>*Interface . The Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions.

As shown in the figure below, the *out**/in*Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in*/out* which has the same*test results and date*Flow Properties but in opposite directions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*******<viewpoint name>** - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization of<span> </span></span>Interface <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">that has the same features as its original Interface except that its</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span>Flow Properties<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>are reversed (conjugated). </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;*</span> Interface</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click one or more interfaces and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface</strong>.<br />The<span> </span>Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">As shown in the figure below, the <em>out</em></span><em>/in</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in<em>/out</em> which has the same </span><em>test results and date </em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Flow Properties but in opposite directions. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="conjugated_interface_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with conjugated Interfaces" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><hr /><p><strong>* </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong> - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926520 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01226: Working with CV-5 typical table

- page_id: `191926520`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926520/Working+with+CV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-19T14:11:33.480+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627926007

627926020

627931615

**On this page**

627926010

##### Creating CV-5 typical table

The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the CV-5 typical table table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 typical table

To create an empty CV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627926006

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae3d9710-8f3c-41dd-9fe0-ea64577c220f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926007</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02b19f1f-9244-4fa5-a6d6-32e0d9f05038"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1ef6059-4e8e-4453-afcc-2147b9ab361d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627931615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c81faddc-31ed-4f8d-923a-5cf808482dfd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bb78b52-7bea-4f5a-82ca-0734b04f8899"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926010</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating CV-5 typical table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</p><p>Resources are related to Capabilities using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the table cell is filled.</p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the CV-5 typical table table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a CV-5 typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a CV-5 typical table</span></h4><p>To create an empty CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong> table.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f02f439-4c17-411c-8176-39a1534fb195"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926006</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927394 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01227: Working with Date and Time Summary Table

- page_id: `191927394`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927394/Working+with+Date+and+Time+Summary+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.716+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Summary and Overview
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1726920497

1726920518

1726920508

The **Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)** depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.

Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.

To add existing model elements

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

The selected date-time elements are added as rows.

1726920495

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR2']

The <strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b7b60e6-1344-45fe-bb0b-299147c13860"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><p>The selected date-time elements are added as rows.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8290fc10-3a7c-4366-8b93-3f90c517d309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927376 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01228: Working with Dictionary diagram

- page_id: `191927376`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927376/Working+with+Dictionary+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-25T09:34:37.667+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Architecture Management > Architecture Management Information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Dictionary (Am-If)** provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.

To create a new or add an existing term

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Dictionary (Am-If)</strong> provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new or add an existing term</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928092 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01229: Working with Environment diagram

- page_id: `191928092`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928092/Working+with+Environment+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.276+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Parameters Pm
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

380372338

380372340

1342655392

**On this page**

380372339

The **Environment** diagram shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Environment diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Environment diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create an Environment diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Environment**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Environment**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Location element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Location in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Location and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Location in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Structure (or Taxonomy, Connectivity, Constraints) package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Location and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_location.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connect the Location

When you have the Location created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

380372336

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Performable Under Condition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Condition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Environment' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Location' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Property Set' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Asset' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Condition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Property' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location Holder' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be3812e6-d9cc-4e90-bee8-b472fac82b93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">380372338</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd03e931-699a-44f4-b8cf-d4e852b29535"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">380372340</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48966f82-7bbf-43f4-8e62-5c8d4f5c0cf0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1342655392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d6be970-c6d3-42f3-add3-8176908f3b6e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90232344-4d31-4e5b-be2d-cd7c7d0052b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">380372339</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Environment</strong> diagram shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12714bf1-3b94-4a07-ba5d-ce96b21c9f36"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Environment diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Environment diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Environment diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Environment</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="344" ac:width="309"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_environment_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Environment </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Location element, but the same is valid for other elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Location in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Location and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="312" ac:width="395"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_environment_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Location in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Structure (or Taxonomy, Connectivity, Constraints) package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Location and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="272" ac:width="318"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_environment_element_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="256" ac:width="649"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_location.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Connect the Location</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have the Location created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="522318a9-11d1-4174-b425-40f62ad1a382"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_palette_location.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="303" ac:width="402"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_locations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Environment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To make an element abstract</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To view the inherited properties</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8069221d-467d-4cac-8285-3db4e66e44f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">380372336</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity Performable Under Condition" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Condition" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Environment" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Location" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Asset" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Condition" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" ri:content-title="Location" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Location Holder" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928545 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 01230: Working with exchanges

- page_id: `191928545`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928545/Working+with+exchanges
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.588+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

##### Showing or hiding realized <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the conveyed<viewpoint name>*Exchange Item on the element realizing<viewpoint name>*Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized <viewpoint name>*Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR2'] :
  1. If the<viewpoint name>* Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Show Realized <viewpoint name>*Exchanges . Click it to hide the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem.
  2. If the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide Realized<viewpoint name>***Exchanges** . Click it to hide the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one <viewpoint name>*Exchange, the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple <viewpoint name>* Exchanges, the <viewpoint name>* Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the <viewpoint name>* Exchanges whose <viewpoint name>* Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the <viewpoint name>*Exchanges whose <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_operational_exchanges.png']

When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide thearrow notation on the realizing elements. The**Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Exchange Item is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose **Options**> **Project**. The **Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to **General**> **Diagrams.**
3. In the **Diagrams**property group, find the **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** option and set it to true.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

##### Dealing with Exchanges after Reconnecting Connectors

When you need to reconnect the connector from one element to another, there are several possible options regarding the exchanges:

- None (default)
- Create New <viewpoint name>* Exchange
- Realize Compatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges
- Update End of <viewpoint name>* Exchange
- Remove Incompatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

###### None

Choosing this option, the incompatible exchanges where source and target does not comply with new source and target, will be removed. This option is default.

###### Create New <viewpoint name>* Exchange

When you reconnect theconnectorto a different element, a shortcut menu opens. In the shortcut menu, you can select to create a new <viewpoint name>* Exchange. The**Realized <viewpoint name>* Exchange** wizardsopens where you can create a new Exchange to be realized instead of the previously realized exchanges.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realize Compatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

When you reconnect the connector to a different element, a shortcut menu opens. In this shortcut menu, you can select to realize the exchanges, which exist between the sender and the new receiver. If the connector links ports, the conveyed items of the resulting exchanges would be ensured to adhere the flow properties on both ends of the connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Update End of <viewpoint name>* Exchange

Choosing this option, the end of the exchange will be updated to match the new sender or new receiver on the reconnected connector's end.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Remove Incompatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

Visible only when the sender and receiver are not changed after reconnection, but the exchanges that were already realized become incompatible, meaning they no longer comply with the new flow properties on both ends of the connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

In the shortcut menu, you can select to create a new &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange. The </span><strong>Realized &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</strong> wizards<span> opens where you can create a new Exchange to be realized instead of the previously realized exchanges.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_create_new_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h4>Realize Compatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</h4><p><span>When you reconnect the connector to a different element, a shortcut menu opens.</span> In this shortcut menu, you can select to realize the exchanges, which exist between the sender and the new receiver. If the connector links ports, the conveyed items of the resulting exchanges would be ensured to adhere the flow properties on both ends of the connector.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_realize_compatible_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Update End of &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</h4><p>Choosing this option, the end of the exchange will be updated to match the new sender or new receiver on the reconnected connector's end.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_update_connector_end.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Remove Incompatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</h4><p>Visible only when the sender and receiver are not changed after reconnection, but the exchanges that were already realized become incompatible, meaning they no longer comply with the new flow properties on both ends of the connector.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_remove_incompatible_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).</span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Sequences diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" ## PAGE 01231: Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `191927891`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927891/Working+with+Functions+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-23T08:39:17.571+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282779718

1282779730

1282779720

The rows of the Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

##### Creating Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To create a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

##### Mapping Function to Operational Activity in Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1282779717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928475 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01232: Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table

- page_id: `191928475`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928475/Working+with+hierarchical+Actual+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-09T10:19:44.485+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and define values. When you add a Property Set to the table, all the existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope.

For your convenience, it is advised to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] first.

To create <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

- Do one of the following:
  - Right-click the <viewpoint name> Parameters package and choose Create Diagram > <viewpoint name> Actual Measurements Table .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select **<domain name> Actual Measurements Table**.
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Typical Measurements Table's]]> toolbar, click the **Evaluate Measurements** button.

To work with <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the Select Element dialog based on the defined scope and whether they have measurements. The new Actual Property Sets with their corresponding structure recursively are created.
  - Click the **Add Existing**button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose existing Property Sets. In the **Select Element** dialog, elements are offered according to the selected perspective - Type, Instance, or Any element.If the Type radio button is selected, all the appropriate Property Sets with instances and are not added in the table yet.If the Instance radio button is selected, all the appropriate instances according to the Property Set scope and not added to the table yet. The existing Actual Property Sets are added to the table with their corresponding structure recursively.
2. Double-click the Measure, Start Date, or End Date cell to add desired values. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### TIP: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain in the Containment tree. The Actual Property Sets will be collected to the table recursively.

To add missing measurements

- Do one of the following:
  - In the table, choose an element for which you want to add missing measurements, and in the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update Selected Actual Property Set . The existing measurements are added for the selected Actual Property Set if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for the selected Actual Property Set.
  - In the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update All Actual Property Sets . The existing measurements are added for all the Actual Property Sets if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for all the Actual Property Sets.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related domains**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects Parameters' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Parameters' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Security Parameters' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Services Parameters' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standards Parameters' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928469 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01233: Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `191928469`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928469/Working+with+hierarchical+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.429+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: TIP

742058609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: TIP

742058622

939865744

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: TIP

742058611

The hierarchical table for typical measurements allows working with the measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing property sets and create measurements for them. Moreover, define measurement types, default values, and documentation directly in the table.

##### Working with Typical Measurements Table

To work with <domain name> Typical Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the drop-down list according to the domain in which the table is created.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set. The new row in the table is created.
2. Select Property Set and in the table toolbar, click the Add New Measurement button.
3. Double-click the Type, Default Value, or Documentation cell add the desired measurement to specify it. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### INFO: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain from the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An earlier version of the table for Typical Measurements vs. new hierarchical table for Typical Measurements

##### Display modes

There are three display modes available for the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table:

- Display Structure. Choose this mode to show all owned measurements in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure.png']
- Display Structure with owned Measurements Recursively . Choose this mode to show all owned measurements recursively in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure_owned_recursively.png']
- Display List of Measurements Recursively. Choose to show elements with all their measurements in a hierarchical order. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_list_recursively.png']

To choose a display mode

1. In the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] .
2. In the shortcut menu, click Display Mode and then choose one of the available modes.

##### Evaluating measurements

After the Typical Measurements Table is created and filled out, you can create an Actual Measurements Table.

To create an Actual Measurements Table from the Typical Measurements Table

- In the filled-out Typical Measurements Table's toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. The hierarchical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] is created.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Typical Measurements Table.

To generate the report from the table toolbar

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

The report is generated with all the data displayed in the table.

To generate the report from the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the desired package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Generate Report > UAF > <more> > Typical Measurements Excel Spreadsheet .
3. Define the name and select a location for your report.
4. Click Save .
5. In the open question dialog, choose whether you want to view the report after it is generated. If you choose to view it, the report is opened afterward.

In the report, the Property Sets with at least one measurement are collected from the chosen package.

If you need to include more packages as the report scope, then use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR2']**.

To generate the report from the Report Wizard

1. In the main menu, click Tools and then chose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR2']**Report Wizard**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, expand the UAF package and choose the Typical Measurements Table template.
3. Click Next .
4. Select the report data and click Next .
5. Select the desired scope (particular package or entire model).
6. Click Next .
7. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
8. Click Generate .

- If the chosen scope contains a single open hierarchical Typical Measurements table, the report is generated from the data of that table.
- If the chosen scope contains more than one open table, the generated report contains the data from all scope.

742058607
