<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927120 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01234: Working with L1 Node Types Table

- page_id: `191927120`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927120/Working+with+L1+Node+Types+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-23T13:57:57.918+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Node Types****Table (L1)**provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.

To add a new element in the Node Types table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose from the following:
  - Known Resource
  - Operational Architecture
  - Operational Mitigation
  - Operational Performer A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.

To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Element dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> <strong>Node Types</strong></span><strong> Table (L1)</strong><span> </span>provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new element in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose from the following:<ul><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Architecture</li><li>Operational Mitigation</li><li>Operational Performer<br />A new row is added.</li></ul></li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Element </strong>dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927185 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01235: Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927185`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927185/Working+with+L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.567+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

INLINE

911370604

INLINE

478104274

INLINE

**On this page**

911370603

INLINE

##### Creating a diagram

To create an L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2-L3 package. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**.
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the L2-L3 package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- A L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Connect the Operational Performers</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="522318a9-11d1-4174-b425-40f62ad1a382"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_smart.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To make an element abstract</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_performer_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To view the inherited properties</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><div><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_performer_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></div><div><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></div><div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8add95f5-ec84-44e9-acfe-35f166e99d82"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</li><li>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928561 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01236: Working with matrices

- page_id: `191928561`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928561/Working+with+matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.836+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['reset', 'predefined', 'matrix', 'matrices']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

For more generic procedures on working with matrices, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR2'] chapter.

##### Building matrices

To build a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope .
2. Specify the Column Scope .
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

##### Mapping elements in matrices

To map elements

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

##### Resetting to default

When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, e.g. change the row or columns element type, dependency criteria, directions. You can restore the following settings to default at any time:

- Description
- Direction
- Dependency Criteria
- Show Elements
- Column Element Type
- Row Element Type

To restore the default predefined matrix settings

- In the matrix toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] and choose the Reset to Default command.

##### Description area

The matrix descriptions in the Description area comes from two different locations: tag value orstereotype.

If the tag value is set, the matrix description is the information from tag value. If the tag value is not set, the description come from the matrix stereotype. The information in the matrix stereotype is always more recent.

If you want to see more recent matrix description (which may change with a new modeling tool/plugin release), clear the tag value. For more informaton about editing tagged values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MD2024xR2'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926932 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01238: Working with NCV-5 typical table

- page_id: `191926932`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926932/Working+with+NCV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-19T14:12:56.022+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

628010590

628010604

628010588

**On this page**

628010594

##### Creating NCV-5 typical table

The context of the NCV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the NCV-5 typical table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 typical table

To create an empty NCV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

628015084

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NCV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</p><p>Resources are related to Capabilities using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the table cell is filled.</p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a NCV-5 typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a NCV-5 typical</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>NCV-5</strong> <strong>Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>NCV-5</strong> <strong>Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An<em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd85468-a942-4243-ae40-7864a6e589ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628015084</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927002 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01239: Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927002`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927002/Working+with+NSOV-3+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-10-21T13:25:56.139+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **NSOV-3****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>NSOV-3 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927603 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01240: Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `191927603`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927603/Working+with+Operational+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-23T08:35:06.259+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282484386

1282484407

1282484397

The Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Activity to a Capability

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282484385

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927505 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01241: Working with Operational Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927505`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927505/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.470+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Operational Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Operational Connectivity and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges

Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange . Click another Operational Performer to connect. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Operational Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations

When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create new Operational Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Operational Performers.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_op_exchange.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="707fdfcb-d53e-4825-bd53-df7c62c87aa2" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p>To create an Operational Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:</span><ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Operational Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Operational Connectivity </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p>An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and <span>Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</span></p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><h3>Creating Operational Exchanges</h3><p>Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Information</span>, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<span><br /></span><ul><li><span>On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange</span>.<span> Click another Operational Performer to connect.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li><span> In the diagram palette, c</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">lick <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.</span><span> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span><br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16de18c6-2f39-40a4-a2f2-b9b40b154023"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4c4befc-85e4-439e-84bb-0c6c8f57299d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations</h3><p>When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:</p><ul><li>Opening Exchange Manager.</li><li>Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.</li></ul><h4>Opening the Exchange Manager</h4><p>To create new Operational Exchange on the Association</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the Association between Operational Performers.</li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_op_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1810a3e-531e-49fa-b3c9-57d324bb8ed3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_on_association.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open an <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">an Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li>In the smart manipulator, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange Manager"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_manager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.<br />The <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_manager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.</p><h4>Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association</h4><p>You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_1_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In the wizard, specify the needed information. </p><p>In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927533 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01242: Working with Operational Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927533`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927533/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-06T15:18:24.558+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An **Operational Connectivity Table** addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An <strong>Operational Connectivity Table</strong> addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927539 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01243: Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `191927539`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927539/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-28T06:15:52.005+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

##### Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927537 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01244: Working with Operational Constraints table

- page_id: `191927537`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927537/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-04T08:36:45.044+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and in the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information, Operational Exchange).
3. In the **Rule** **Specification**cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Operational Constraint for one constraint.

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row is added. </span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Operational Exchange).</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Rule</strong> <strong>Specification </strong>c<span style="color: Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify body.<br /></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da8deb6e-f7c6-45c5-9113-b42000f6e9a1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="auto-cursor-target" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the <strong>Rule Kind</strong> from one of the following:</span></span></span><ul><li>Structural Assertion</li><li>Action Assertion</li><li>Derivation</li><li>Contract</li><li>Constraint</li><li>Guidance</li><li>Security Policy</li><li>Caveat</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3cdf45c8-89fb-4a00-9e31-dad41a4aacbc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can assign more than one Operational Constraint for one constraint.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In <strong>Select Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select Operational Constraints.<span> The filled row is added in table.  </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927598 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01245: Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927598`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927598/Working+with+Operational+High+Level+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:27:32.133+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927520 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01247: Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927520`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927520/Working+with+Operational+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:06:16.100+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

##### Creating Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

The owner of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram must be an Operational Performer.

To create an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

Create the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Composite Structure element using any method that is most suitable for you:

- Right-click the Composite element in a Structure Diagram and Create Diagram.
- Right-click the Composite element in the Containment Tree and Create Diagram.
- Select the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Smart Manipulator.

The diagram creation wizard is displayed, showing the hierarchical list of part properties owned by the selected composite element. All the elements or any subset can be included in the diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector between a pair of Operational Roles, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector .
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 01248: Working with Operational Parametric diagram

- page_id: `191927547`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927547/Working+with+Operational+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

495805059

495805061

495805060

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

495805058

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

It contains </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</span></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7bc1f2b-cbc9-40c5-a168-836e1ff80e4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">495805058</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927605 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01249: Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `191927605`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927605/Working+with+Operational+Performers+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-06-04T08:08:11.552+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****displays the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Performers, Operational Architecturesand their specializations, andthe Columns areCapabilities.

To create the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Performers, Operational Architectures).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] Refresh button.

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Performer to a Capability

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map Operational Performers to a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
