<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927552 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01250: Working with Operational Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `191927552`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927552/Working+with+Operational+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:09:47.670+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] must be created first.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Distress Signal Handling*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Operational Process Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Operational Activity Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Operational Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlane' space='EAIP']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4598fdbb-627c-4404-83a3-a9492c7ef040"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Processes diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Process diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create an Operational Process Flow diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe66ee0e-a6e8-4ac0-9ef0-d8d15b0b2a29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.</li></ul></li><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions wit<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Let's say you have a defined structure and </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">create the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element - </span><em>Distress Signal Handling</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> as a Swimlane representative.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_process_flow.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Process Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Operational </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Process Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Operational Process Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create an Operational Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Swimlane, select a needed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Activity Action or Operational Action</span>.</li><li>In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational </span>Process Flow.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_process_flow_representatives.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Process Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52299b42-76e1-4f81-b053-f6471a46dd6e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.</li><li>If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the empty diagram will be created.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />A new <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational </span>Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="EAIP" ri:content-title="Swimlane" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927551 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01251: Working with Operational Processes diagram

- page_id: `191927551`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927551/Working+with+Operational+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:26:31.974+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927534 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01252: Working with Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927534`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927534/Working+with+Operational+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T09:10:12.922+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

1. In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927557 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01253: Working with Operational Sequences diagram

- page_id: `191927557`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927557/Working+with+Operational+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:06:52.153+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Operational Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram palette, select the Operational Message.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927565 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01254: Working with Operational Structure diagram

- page_id: `191927565`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927565/Working+with+Operational+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.662+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f41af3d-e6d2-477f-bbb1-584fb0424899" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Structure and d</span>o one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Operational Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="257" ac:width="309"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_operational_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Operational Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span>. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="331" ac:width="560"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="473" ac:width="365"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="313" ac:width="797"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_composition_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="295" ac:width="847"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="316" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.</p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="221" ac:width="383"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="126" ac:width="501"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="279" ac:width="502"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d13e10d-1e94-4838-bbd0-5cc2c6d0a234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the <strong>Operational Association</strong> also.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="887" ac:width="727"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd4c464-ed89-4754-b3b4-2f0c0451e567"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="773" ac:width="747"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927587 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01255: Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927587`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927587/Working+with+Operational+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.714+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

911370604

478104274

**On this page**

911370603

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Operational Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Taxonomy**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Taxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- An Operational Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

## PAGE 01256: Working with Operational Taxonomy table

- page_id: `191927599`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:27:58.343+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, or Operational Performer. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Element dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p> To create an Operational Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, or Operational Performer. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926787 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01257: Working with OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926787`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926787/Working+with+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-29T08:38:30.029+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414455593

414455596

414455592

**On this page**

414455595

##### Creating OV-2 diagram

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Architectures.
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performers.
3. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connector.
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connector.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.
  - On the Operational Exchanges toolbar in the diagram palette, click the Operational Exchange button and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.
3. Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3da90bc1-80c5-4034-a9d7-4667bdea132d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455593</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a14cf15-7ecd-439b-b8f2-7d04b43d3f53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455596</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da8c3f64-4014-4d9d-ada5-7ba55fc4899a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455592</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d2e7ae4-38ea-4e93-91fd-966e796b942b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7b70ff-1287-4ca5-b5fa-a10e962bb602"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455595</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating OV-2 diagram</h3><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Architectures.</li><li>Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational  Performers.</li><li>Associate Operational  Roles using Operational Connector.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connector.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>. To open the wizard, do either:<ul><li>Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.</li><li>On the <strong>Operational Exchanges</strong> toolbar in the diagram palette, click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button and then click the Operational Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926792 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01258: Working with OV-3 table

- page_id: `191926792`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926792/Working+with+OV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:24:00.158+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-3 table

1. Add existing Operational Exchanges.
2. Click the Add Existing button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connector. In case a Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.

- You need to use an OV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.
- You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-3 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add existing Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connector. In case a Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24c45741-9173-4ab5-b2f7-90d1adf18b9d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to use an OV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.</li><li>You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926795 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01259: Working with OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `191926795`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926795/Working+with+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:25:57.532+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-4 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.

You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.

You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an OV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before SV-1 view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><p>You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</p><p>You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.</p><p>You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an OV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</p><p>Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before SV-1 view.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926804 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01260: Working with OV-6a table

- page_id: `191926804`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926804/Working+with+OV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:30:30.822+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following

- You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.
- You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Operational Constraint</strong> button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the<strong> Add Existing Operational Constraint</strong> button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="301160f6-1ced-453f-b022-3d77f727858d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.</li><li>You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926813 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01261: Working with OV-7 diagram

- page_id: `191926813`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926813/Working+with+OV-7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:54:58.600+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-7 Information Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-7 diagram

1. Create Entity Items.
2. Add Properties.
3. Draw Associations.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Entity Items.</li><li>Add Properties.</li><li>Draw Associations.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927280 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01262: Working with P1 Resource Types Table

- page_id: `191927280`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927280/Working+with+P1+Resource+Types+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-23T14:00:50.450+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Resource Types****Table (P1)**provides all the available resource types.

To add a new element in the Resource Types table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose from the following:
  - Capability Configuration
  - Known Resource
  - Natural Resource
  - Organization
  - Person
  - Post
  - Project
  - Resource Architecture
  - Resource Mitigation
  - Resource Service
  - Responsibility
  - Security Enclave
  - Software
  - System
  - Technology A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.

To add an existing element in the Resource Types table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Resource Performer dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Resource Architecture, Resource Mitigation, Resource Service, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, Technology).
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added Resource Performer.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> <strong>Resource Types</strong></span><strong><span> </span>Table (P1)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available resource types.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new element in the Resource Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose from the following:<ul><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Natural Resource</li><li>Organization</li><li>Person</li><li>Post</li><li>Project</li><li>Resource Architecture</li><li>Resource Mitigation</li><li>Resource Service</li><li>Responsibility</li><li>Security Enclave</li><li>Software</li><li>System</li><li>Technology<br />A new row is added.</li></ul></li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing element in the Resource Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Resource Performer </strong>dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Resource Architecture, Resource Mitigation, Resource Service, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, Technology).</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added Resource Performer.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928564 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01263: Working with Parametric diagram

- page_id: `191928564`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928564/Working+with+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.886+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

492938369

492938371

492938370

In all Parametric diagrams you can:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . To perform this step, the should be installed.

defineTo define the parametric formula structure

1. On a diagram, display or create Measurement properties (Measurements will be used as arguments or result of calculation). The owner of the Measurements will be the same as for the diagram.
2. Define types for the measurements (e.g. Primitive types like Integer, Real.)
3. Create the Constraint Property which type should be Constraint Block (create new or select existing).
4. Once type is set, define the calculation formula in the curly brackets in the diagram.
5. Create the Constraint Parameters for each member of the formula in the Constraint Property.
6. Use Binding Connector to connect Measurements to the proper Constraint Parameter.

For more information about Parametric diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

When the formula structure is defined, you can initiate the simulation.

simulateTo simulate parametrics

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] the Parametric diagram owner (e.g. Operational Performer).
2. After the instances are created, define the slot values of the Measurements representing arguments in the formula.
3. Select the created instance and from the shortcut menu, select Simulation > Run . The simulation process is initiated.

If you want to simulate more than one instance, you can do that in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024xR2'].

The predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] can be applied to the UAF elements.

492938367

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling parametric constraints' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927744 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01264: Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart

- page_id: `191927744`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927744/Working+with+Personnel+Availability+Gantt+Chart
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.432+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: ['personnel', 'availability', 'gantt', 'chart', 'fill-post', 'actual-post', 'actual-organization', 'actual-person']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

With the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, you will be able to model your actual organization structure based on the typical organization structure and fill Actual Posts with one or more Actual Persons. You will be able to visualize how the actual posts are filled, see which actual persons become available on a certain period of time.

The Personnel Availability Gantt Chart shows Actual Organizations and Actual Posts as rows. Each Actual Post can be filled by an Actual Person. When the Actual Post is filled by an Actual Person, the Fills Post relationship is created. The start and end dates can be defined for the Fills Post relationship. The start and end dates define the time frame for Actual Person filling the Actual Post. The same Actual Person can work in several positions.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR2'] chapter.

##### Creating Personnel Availability Gantt Chart

When you choose to create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** opens.

To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart from the wizard

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Personnel Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_personnel_gantt_chart.png']
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Personnel Availability Gantt Chart** check box.

To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** is disabled

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Personnel Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
2. Type the chart name. (Optional)
3. Click Finish .

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Organizations and Actual Posts

To add a new Actual Organization

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Organization . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_actual_organization.png']
2. Select a type for the Actual Organization. An empty line for the newly created Actual Organization appears on the diagram pane.

To add a new Actual Post

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Post . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_actual_post.png']
2. Select a type for the Actual Post. An empty line for the newly created Actual Post appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Actual Organization

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Actual Organization .
2. In the Select Actual Organization dialog, select an Actual Organization and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add an existing Actual Post

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Actual Post .
2. In the Select Actual Post dialog, select an Actual Post and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Sub Actual Organizations and Sub Actual Posts

To add a new Sub Actual Organizations

1. Select the Actual Organizations for which you want to create a Sub Actual Organizations.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Actual Organizations .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Organizations, select Add New Sub Actual Organizations .

To add a new Sub Actual Post

1. Select the Actual Post for which you want to create a Sub Actual Post.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Actual Post .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Post, select Add New Sub Actual Post .

To add an existing Sub Actual Organizations

1. Select the Actual Organizations for which you want to add a Sub Actual Organizations.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Actual Organizations .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Organization, select Add Existing Sub Actual Organizations .
3. From the Select Actual Organizations dialog, select an Actual Organizations and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To add an existing Sub Actual Post

1. Select the Actual Post for which you want to add a Sub Actual Post.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Actual Post .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Post, select Add Existing Sub Actual Post .
3. From the Select Actual Post dialog, select an Actual Post and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Actual Person

To add an Actual Person

1. Select an Actual Post for which you want to assign an Actual Person.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Person .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Actual Post and select Add Actual Person . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_actual_person.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you want to see each Actual Person in the separate row, in the chart Options area, select **Display Actual Person in Different Lines** check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Editing Actual Person

To edit an Actual Person

1. Right-click an Actual Post for which you want to edit an Actual Person.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Actual Person and click the Actual Person you want to edit. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_actual_person.png']
3. Edit the Start or End dates.
4. Click OK .

##### Removing Actual Person

To remove an Actual Person

1. Right-click an Actual Post for which you want to remove an Actual Person.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Remove Actual Person and click the Actual Person you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_actual_person.png']

##### Modifying dates for Actual Person

You can modify the time frame of the Actual Person filling the Actual Post. You can do so in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2']**Specification** window of the particular Fills Post relationship or directly in the chart.

It is quick and easy to modify dates directly in the chart:

- To change the start date, simply drag the Actual Person bar to the left or right.
- To change the end date, slide the end of the Actual Person bar to the left or right.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Displaying all suppressed or removed from the chart

When an Actual Organization or Actual Post is displayed in *italic*, it means that some sub elements were removed from the chart. You can restore them back with the click of one button.

To display all sub elements removed from the chart

1. Select the Actual Organization (or Actual Post) which sub elements you want to restore.
2. In the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

The **Display All** button also expands the suppressed structure.

##### Deleting elements and sub elements

You can either remove the element or sub element from the chart or delete it from the model.

To delete an element or sub elementfrom the chart

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete an element or sub elementfrom the model

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

You will be able to visualize how the actual posts are filled, see which actual persons become available on a certain period of time.</p><p>The Personnel Availability Gantt Chart shows Actual Organizations and Actual Posts as rows. Each Actual Post can be filled by an Actual Person. When the Actual Post is filled by an Actual Person, the Fills Post relationship is created. The start and end dates can be defined for the Fills Post relationship. The start and end dates define the time frame for Actual Person filling the Actual Post. The same Actual Person can work in several positions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a283d7-ac16-44af-bffb-bfda138b8c03"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To increase the diagram readability, use legends.
