<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927713 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01265: Working with Personnel Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927713`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927713/Working+with+Personnel+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T16:06:35.962+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Connectivity diagram.

To create a typical form of Personnel Connectivity diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, and Persons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual form of Personnel Connectivity diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Connectivity diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a typical form of Personnel Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual form of Personnel Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927718 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01266: Working with Personnel Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927718`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927718/Working+with+Personnel+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-31T07:47:21.458+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Personnel Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in an Personnel Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Personnel Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in an Personnel Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927722 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01267: Working with Personnel Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `191927722`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927722/Working+with+Personnel+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-28T06:32:08.184+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Constraints Definition diagram.

To create a typical form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, and Persons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Constraints Definition diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a typical form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927723 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01268: Working with Personnel Constraints table

- page_id: `191927723`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927723/Working+with+Personnel+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-28T08:19:26.726+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding a new Resource

1. Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the Select Subject Of Resource Constraint dialog, choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Data Element, or Resource Exchange.
3. Define the **Rule Specification**. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the Rule Kind from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.

To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding and existing Resource

- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding a new Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog, choose <span>Resource Artifact,</span> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Data Element, or Resource Exchange</span>. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Define the <strong>Rule Specification</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d7218cc-0982-4a1e-a68c-e9dde5ebd074"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Select the <strong>Rule Kind</strong> from one of the following:<ul><li>Structural Assertion</li><li>Action Assertion</li><li>Derivation</li><li>Contract</li><li>Constraint</li><li>Guidance</li><li>Security Policy</li><li>Caveat</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10bb7bd-1c36-41bc-a773-c181b23d6555"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding and existing Resource</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927739 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01269: Working with Personnel Evolution diagram

- page_id: `191927739`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927739/Working+with+Personnel+Evolution+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-12T14:07:24.649+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Personnel Evolution diagram

To create a Personnel Evolution diagram

1. Create new Version Of Configuration:
  1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
  2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.
2. Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration:
  1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
  2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
  3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

##### Removing Resources from Personnel Evolution diagram

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from Personnel Evolution diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01270: Working with Personnel Forecast diagram

- page_id: `191927741`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927741/Working+with+Personnel+Forecast+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-05T12:08:20.736+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658918461

1658918466

1658918465

To create a Personnel Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658918459

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927714 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01271: Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927714`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927714/Working+with+Personnel+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:15:02.449+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

The Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be completed.

To create a Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0d3b6fc-5652-46bb-a6e2-79c0a027c769" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creating Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram</h3><p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d12d1409-0664-4058-b64a-55b2789b31c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><p>If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>Finish<span> </span></strong>when you are done. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette_personnel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="187a6268-a1ef-4338-8b64-da33c1c1e86f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47a99e66-cfcd-4790-a2e5-5c20d807712f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Learn more</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</strong> You can</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> check the consistency of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927726 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01272: Working with Personnel Parametric diagram

- page_id: `191927726`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927726/Working+with+Personnel+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.367+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496255768

496255770

496255769

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Organizational Resources (e.g. Organization, Post, Person). It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Organizational Resources context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496255767

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Organizational Resources context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6044e446-b71f-4a29-ab7c-d6c9b699e33c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496255767</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927730 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01273: Working with Personnel Processes diagram

- page_id: `191927730`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927730/Working+with+Personnel+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T08:39:12.983+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Personnel Processes diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Operational Processes and PersonnelStructure diagrams should be created first.

To create a Personnel Processes diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from Personnel Structure, Personnel Taxonomy diagrams.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Operational Processes diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Personnel Processes diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> and Personnel<span class="confluence-link"> Structure</span> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Personnel Processes diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> Personnel Structure, Personnel Taxonomy diagrams.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927731 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01274: Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `191927731`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927731/Working+with+Personnel+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.391+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Personnel Processes Flow diagram

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] must be created first.

To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram for Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Organizational Resource or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Function Action or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Function Action wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Personnel Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Organization UK SAR Organization*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Personnel Process Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Function Action or Resource Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Personnel Process Flow diagram for a Function Action or Resource Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Function Action or Resource Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Function Action or Resource Action.
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Personnel Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new Personnel Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein the Personnel Processes Flow diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).<br /></span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927719 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01275: Working with Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927719`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927719/Working+with+Personnel+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:31:06.597+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill in a Role-based Personnel Connectivity Table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a PersonnelRole-based Connectivity Table

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can fill in a Role-based Personnel Connectivity Table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a Personnel </span>Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fa275e3-6096-4d07-a83e-cee5dd4f2328"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span>Exchanges </span>Identifier, Resource <span>Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927754 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01276: Working with Personnel Sequences diagram

- page_id: `191927754`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927754/Working+with+Personnel+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:15:51.825+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in the Personnel Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram palette, select the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Sequences diagram" class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927762 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01277: Working with Personnel Structure diagram

- page_id: `191927762`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927762/Working+with+Personnel+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.556+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Personnel Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Personnel Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Personnel Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Personnel Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Organization element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Organization in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Organization and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Organization in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Personnel Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Organization and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_organization.png']

Organization

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Organization (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Organizations

When you have Organization created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Composition** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Resource****Association.**
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Resource </strong><strong>Association.</strong></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="787" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927779 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01278: Working with Personnel Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927779`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927779/Working+with+Personnel+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.587+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

486025715

486025718

486025714

**On this page**

486025717

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Personnel Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the PersonnelTaxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**PersonnelTaxonomy**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **PersonnelTaxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Organization element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Organization (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Personnel Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Organization (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_organization.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Organization (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connect the elements

When you have Organization (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927788 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01279: Working with Personnel Taxonomy table

- page_id: `191927788`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927788/Working+with+Personnel+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:35:06.003+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Personnel Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Organization, Person, Post, or Responsibility. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Organizational Resource dialog, select the desired Organizational Resources. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Organizational Resource</strong> dialog, select the desired Organizational Resources. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928327 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01280: Working With Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `191928327`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928327/Working+With+Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-30T10:27:39.833+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Coverage analysis
- labels: ['create-metric-table', 'show-metric-suites', 'hide-metric-suites', 'calculate-new-metric', 'recalculate', 'add-new-metric-with-different-parameters', 'show-abbreviation-in-column-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

450242730

450242732

450242731

In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.

To create a metric table

1. In the Containment tree, select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working With Predefined Metric Suites' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] for the metric table.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The metric suites in the **Create Metric Table** dialog will be listed according to your selected package.
3. Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the **Options** button and select the **Show Abbreviation in Column Name**option.

When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.

To show or hide metric suites in the metric table

1. In the metric table toolbar, click the Show Metric Suites button.
2. Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.

There are three actions with the metrics. You can:

- Calculate New Metric . Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.
- Recalculate . Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.
- Add New Metric with Different Parameters . Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.

450242729

*****packagesIn the following table, you will find the information in which packages you can create a metric table for the particular metric suites.

| Metric Suite | Packages |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UAF | DoDAF | MODAF | NAF | NAF4 |  |
| Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects | PersonnelPersonnel TaxonomyPersonnel Structure | Operational ViewpointOV-4 | Operational ViewpointOV-4 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-4 |  |
| Functions Covered by Resource Performers | ResourcesResources TaxonomyResources Structure | Systems ViewpointSV-1SV-2 | Systems ViewpointSV-1SV-2 | NATO Systems ViewpointNSV-1NSV-2 | Physical ResourcesP1 |
| Actual Projects Covered by Milestones | ProjectsProjects StructureProjects RoadmapProjects ConnectivityProjects ParametersProjects ProcessesProjects TaxonomyProjects Traceability | Project ViewpointPV-1PV-2 | Acquisition ViewpointAcV-1AcV-2 | NATO Programme ViewpointNPV-1NPV-2 | LogicalLr |
| Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources | ProjectsProjects StructureProjects RoadmapProjects ConnectivityProjects ParametersProjects ProcessesProjects TaxonomyProjects Traceability | Project ViewpointPV-1PV-2 | Acquisition ViewpointAcV-1AcV-2 | NATO Programme ViewpointNPV-1NPV-2 | LogicalLr |
| Operational Activities Covered by Functions | OperationalOperational TaxonomyOperational Processes | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-5 | LogicalL4 |
| Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers | OperationalOperational Processes | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-5 | LogicalL4 |
| Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers | OperationalOperational Taxonomy | Operational ViewpointOV-2 | Operational ViewpointOV-2 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-2 | LogicalL1 |
| Capabilities Covered by Actual Project | StrategyStrategic StructureStrategic Taxonomy | Capability ViewpointCV-2 | Strategic ViewpointStV-2 | NATO Capability ViewpointNCV-2 | ConceptsC1 |
| Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities | StrategyStrategic Taxonomy | Capability ViewpointCV-2 | Strategic ViewpointStV-2 | NATO Capability ViewpointNCV-2 | ConceptsC1 |
| Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration | StrategyStrategic Taxonomy | Capability ViewpointCV-2 | Strategic ViewpointStV-2 | NATO Capability ViewpointNCV-2 | ConceptsC1 |
| Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers | StrategyStrategic Taxonomy | Capability ViewpointCV-2 | Strategic ViewpointStV-2 | NATO Capability ViewpointNCV-2 | ConceptsC1 |

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96e50ea3-cc95-4745-bebe-ea966558a5ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">450242730</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52602ee7-36a6-4d89-9ae8-75aa2397b07d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">450242732</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7586a54-9513-407a-a439-ac1353779367"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">450242731</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select a <ac:link ac:anchor="packages"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package*]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working With Predefined Metric Suites" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> for the metric table.</li><li><p>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New Metric Table</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_metri_table_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working With Predefined Metric Suites" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb26572c-f307-4e58-919d-593a98b06d54"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The metric suites in the <strong>Create Metric Table</strong> dialog will be listed according to your selected package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</p><p><br /></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbc2d883-7fa9-4baa-8fbe-705bc7349248"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Options</strong> button and select the <strong>Show Abbreviation in Column Name </strong>option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show or hide metric suites in the metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Show Metric Suites</strong> button.</li><li>Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.</li></ol><p>There are three actions with the metrics. You can:</p><ul><li><strong>Calculate New Metric</strong>. Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.</li><li><strong>Recalculate</strong>. Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.</li><li><strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong>. Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e6fc98a-4468-4e71-95cb-2cb48733a427"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">450242729</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>*</strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdf21417-8a20-4888-8f13-ca216c69564c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">packages</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>In the following table, you will find the information in which packages you can create a metric table for the particular metric suites.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 95.5568%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 17.691%;" /><col style="width: 17.174%;" /><col style="width: 14.8765%;" /><col style="width: 15.6232%;" /><col style="width: 15.7381%;" /><col style="width: 18.8972%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th rowspan="2">Metric <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928039 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01281: Working with Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191928039`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928039/Working+with+Project+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-23T08:37:39.045+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282681767

1282681778

1282681777

The Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping (Pj-Tr) shows the mapping between the capabilities and the project activities that those capabilities support directly or using the implied relationship.

The Rows of this matrix are Project Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows **Scope** (Project Activities).
2. Specify Columns **Scope** ( Capabilities ).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Project Activitiesmap to Capabilities using Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping a Project Activity to a Capability

To map a Project Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282681766

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927992 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01283: Working with Projects Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `191927992`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927992/Working+with+Projects+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.645+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1437829688

1437829699

1437829689

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] must be created first.

To create a Project Process Flow diagram for Project Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Project Role to be represented by the Swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Organizational Resource or Project Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Project Activity Actions or drop Project Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Project Activity Actions wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Projects Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ProjectsProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Projects Activity Action. You can create an inner Projects Process Flow diagram for a Projects Activity Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Projects Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Projects Activity Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Projects Activity Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Projects Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Project Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new Projects Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Project Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource or Resource Role and Project Activity (the behavior of the Project Activity Action).

1437829687

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR2']
