In the Swimlane, you can add either Projects Activity Action. You can create an inner Projects Process Flow diagram for a Projects Activity Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a Projects Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Projects Activity Action</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Swimlane, select a needed Projects <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Activity Action</span>.</li><li>In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Projects Process Flow.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Project Role to be represented by the swimlane.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_process_flow_representatives.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Process Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8f13748-4ee0-4144-baa4-a2c8977ae01c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.</li><li>If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the empty diagram will be created.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />A new Projects Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When the Project Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource or Resource Role and Project Activity (the behavior of the Project Activity Action).</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17d3992a-2451-4415-a730-2628b2cd6570"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437829687</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Swimlanes" ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927991 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01284: Working with Projects Processes diagram

- page_id: `191927991`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927991/Working+with+Projects+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-08-06T06:46:10.379+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Project Processes diagram for Project Activity hierarchies

1. Create Project Activities.
2. Create Resource Performers or reuse them from Project Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Resource Performers and Project Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Project Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Project Processes diagram for Project Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Project Activities.</li><li>Create Resource Performers or reuse them from Project Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Resource Performers and Project Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Project Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927997 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01285: Working with Projects Roadmap diagram

- page_id: `191927997`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927997/Working+with+Projects+Roadmap+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.679+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

33

##### Creating Projects Roadmap diagram

When you choose to create Projects Roadmap diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a Projects Roadmap diagram

1. Right-click the Projects Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Projects Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Projects Roadmap** check box.

To create a Projects Roadmap diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Projects Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Projects Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Projects Roadmap diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog, select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project .
2. An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. If you click**Add Existing**without selecting any project, the Actual Project will be added at the end of the project list.
2. An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create an Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right-click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

To add an existing Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to add an existing Actual Sub Project.
2. On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
3. An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in Projects Roadmap diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - Right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### Editing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in Projects Roadmap diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu, select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define the selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change the dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Removing Actual Projects

To remove an Actual Project from the chart

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete an Actual Project from the model

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] step 2 opens.
2. In the bottom right corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Hiding or displaying columns

You can choose to hide or display the Project Name, Start Date, End Date, Duration, and %Completed columns.

To show/hide the columns

1. In the diagram toolbar, click Show Columns.
2. In the menu, select the needed columns.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928003 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01286: Working with Projects Structure diagram

- page_id: `191928003`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928003/Working+with+Projects+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.746+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1147916981

1147916985

1147916980

**On this page**

1147916984

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Projects Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Projects Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Projects Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Projects Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Project in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Project and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Project in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Projects Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Project and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_project.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Projects

When you have Project created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Project Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Aggregation** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association**.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

1147925220

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928020 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01287: Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191928020`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928020/Working+with+Projects+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.915+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

827966462

827966464

827966461

**On this page**

827966463

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Project Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Project Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Project Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_projects_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Project Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Project (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_project.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Project Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Project (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_project.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Project (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span>. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other elements.</span> </p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Project (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="308" ac:width="497"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>Name the element.</span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an element in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Project Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Search for the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Project (or other appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="444" ac:width="357"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_project_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="337" ac:width="862"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Project (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_pallet_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="728"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_generalization_smart.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make an element abstract</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928029 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01288: Working with Projects Taxonomy table

- page_id: `191928029`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928029/Working+with+Projects+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:38:28.031+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Projects Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Project dialog, select the desired projects. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Projects Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select the desired projects. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927799 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01289: Working with Resources Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927799`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927799/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-11-06T14:57:41.621+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Connectivity diagram

The Resources Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be completed.

To create a Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Resources Connectivity product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Resources Structure diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] as soon as the Resources Connectivity has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Organizational Resources.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927811 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01290: Working with Resources Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `191927811`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927811/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T14:45:50.192+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

A Resources Connectivity matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent Resource Exchangesand their headers represent Resources. The arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource Exchangebetween resources. A Resources Connectivity matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

##### Creating Resource Connectivity matrix

To create a Resources Connectivity matrix

1. Specify the **Row Scope** (Resources).
2. Specify the **Column Scope** (Resources).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Connectivity matrix

To add a new Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Exchange appears in the selected intersection.

To remove an existing Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the filled intersection.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Exchange you want to remove from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</span></span>between resources. A Resources Connectivity matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><h3>Creating Resource Connectivity rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Connectivity matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><h3 <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927809 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01291: Working with Resources Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927809`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927809/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-02T15:12:29.794+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Resources Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in an Resources Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Resources Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in an Resources Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01292: Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `191927815`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927815/Working+with+Resources+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-19T11:42:57.995+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Constraints Definition diagram

The Resources Constraints Definition diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be completed.

To create a Resources Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Resource Taxonomy product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model the internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] as soon as the Resources Taxonomy has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Constraints Definition diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect the Organizational Resources
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</p><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as soon as the Resources Taxonomy has been completed.</p><h3>Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect resources.</li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect the Organizational Resources</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2f47c85-d2d3-41e4-8e6a-68dc9af369e2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Resource Information Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927818 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01293: Working with Resources Constraints table

- page_id: `191927818`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927818/Working+with+Resources+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-09T06:06:22.159+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resources Constraints table by adding a new Resource

1. Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the Select Subject Of Resource Constraint dialog, choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Resource Information, or Resource Exchange.
3. Define the **Rule Specification**. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the Rule Kind from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.

To create a Resources Constraints table by adding and existing Resource

- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resources Constraints table by adding a new Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog, choose <span>Resource Artifact,</span> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Resource Information, or Resource Exchange</span>. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Define the <strong>Rule Specification</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d7218cc-0982-4a1e-a68c-e9dde5ebd074"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Select the <strong>Rule Kind</strong> from one of the following:<ul><li>Structural Assertion</li><li>Action Assertion</li><li>Derivation</li><li>Contract</li><li>Constraint</li><li>Guidance</li><li>Security Policy</li><li>Caveat</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10bb7bd-1c36-41bc-a773-c181b23d6555"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Resources Constraints table by adding and existing Resource</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul>
````

## PAGE 01294: Working with Resources Evolution diagram

- page_id: `191927842`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-12T14:09:37.094+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Resources Evolution diagram

To create a Resources Evolution diagram

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

###### newCreate new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Resources Evolution diagram

1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

###### defineDefine the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag the resource onto the Version of Configuration.
  - Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Resources from a Resources Evolution diagram

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Resources Evolution diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927844 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01295: Working with Resources Forecast table

- page_id: `191927844`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927844/Working+with+Resources+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-05T12:09:29.266+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1608782209

1608782211

1608782210

To create a Resources Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1608782206

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927802 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01297: Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927802`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927802/Working+with+Resources+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T09:16:19.392+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

The Resources Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']should be completed.

To create a Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bf57e4d-30da-4b66-a1e0-537397b58313" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creating Resources Internal Connectivity diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resources Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the</span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> should be completed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Resources Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Internal Connectivity diagram</h3><p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in Resources Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click<span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>and then click the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><p>If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span> </span>opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>Finish<span> </span></strong>when you are done. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette_resources.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af13ae21-fbdb-427e-abe0-0943b22dac1a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables you to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b8bd893-010e-4b1e-ba61-1cf6f9c68f89"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Learn more</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</strong> You can</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> check the consistency of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927827 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01298: Working with Resources Parametric diagram

- page_id: `191927827`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927827/Working+with+Resources+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.731+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496491094

496491096

496491095

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Resource Performer (e.g. Capability Configuration, Resource Artifact, System). It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Resource Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496491093

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Resource Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95bf8296-5b2c-47b6-842b-3e7eaa6ca7d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496491093</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927831 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01299: Working with Resources Processes diagram

- page_id: `191927831`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927831/Working+with+Resources+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T07:54:22.475+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Resources Processes diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Operational Processes and Resources Structure diagrams should be created first.

To create a Resources Processes diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from Resource Structure, Resource Taxonomy diagrams.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Operational Processes diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Resources Processes diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> and <span class="confluence-link">Resources Structure</span> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Resources Processes diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> Resource Structure, Resource Taxonomy diagrams.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927832 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01300: Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `191927832`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927832/Working+with+Resources+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.754+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Resources Processes Flow diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Resources Processes diagram should be created first.

##### Creating Resources Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Process Flow diagram for Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Resource Performer or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Function Action or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Function Action wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Resource Process Flow diagram with the top element -*MRT Searcher*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ResourceProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Function Action or Resource Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Resource Process Flow diagram for a Function Action or Resource Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Resource Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Function Action or Resource Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Function Action or Resource Action.
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Resource Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new ResourceProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Resource Performer and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein the Resources Processes Flow diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<span> </span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927810 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01301: Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `191927810`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927810/Working+with+Resources+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-23T15:25:13.204+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table

There should be the realized resource exchanges between Resource Roles in your model.

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can fill in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges.</span></p><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17e00dca-729a-4383-b9de-8a724ec104c1"><ac:rich-text-body>There should be the realized resource exchanges between Resource Roles in your model.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.<span> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927849 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01302: Working with Resources Sequences diagram

- page_id: `191927849`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927849/Working+with+Resources+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T09:16:46.142+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in the Resources Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram palette, select the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Sequences diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Sequences diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram palette, select the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:<ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" data.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section
