````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Processes diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Processes diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54f5f7b4-a6d5-4f3f-9fa1-be50df41fa6f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe66ee0e-a6e8-4ac0-9ef0-d8d15b0b2a29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you click <strong>Cancel </strong>in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Drag the Resource Performer or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.</li></ul></li><li>Create Security Process Actions/Function Actions or drop Security Process/Function from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Security Process Actions/Function Actions wit<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Let's say you have a defined structure and </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">create the Security Process Flow diagram with the top element </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">as a Swimlane representative.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_process_flow.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Security </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Process Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Security Process Action or Function Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Security Process Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Function Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a Security Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Function Action</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Swimlane, select a needed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Security Process Action or Function Action</span>.</li><li>In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Security </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Process Flow.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_process_flow_representatives.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8f13748-4ee0-4144-baa4-a2c8977ae01c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined.</li><li>If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the empty diagram will be created.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />A new <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Security </span>Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Resource Performer and Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Resource Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927936 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01325: Working with Security Processes diagram

- page_id: `191927936`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927936/Working+with+Security+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-04T12:10:13.654+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies

1. Create Security Process
2. Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Security Process</li><li>Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927918 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01326: Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

- page_id: `191927918`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927918/Working+with+Security+Role-based+Connectivity+Operational+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-12T09:25:06.658+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table** specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Operational Roles. You can fill in a table by adding new or existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors.

If you choose to add an Operational Connector, all Operational Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connector

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select the needed Operational Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchanges Identifier, Operational Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchangedata.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities

1. In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Activities button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Activities dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table</strong> specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Operational Roles. You can fill in a table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa3b050a-eccc-49a2-bd41-45a3c042f58d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you choose to add an Operational Connector, all Operational Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connector</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the dialog, </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">select the needed Operational Exchanges.</span></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchanges Identifier, Operational Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Operational <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Activities</strong> button.</li><li>In the open <strong>Producing and Consuming Activities </strong>dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927917 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01327: Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table

- page_id: `191927917`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927917/Working+with+Security+Role-based+Connectivity+Resource+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-12T09:26:02.293+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table** specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Resource Roles. You can fill in a table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors.

If you choose to add a Resource Connector, all Resoutce Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity Table

To add an existing Resource Exchange or Resource Connector

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog select needed Resource Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions

1. In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Functions dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table</strong> specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Resource Roles. You can fill in a table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa3b050a-eccc-49a2-bd41-45a3c042f58d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you choose to add a Resource Connector, all Resoutce Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange or Resource Connector</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the dialog select needed Resource Exchanges.</span></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.</li><li>In the open <strong>Producing and Consuming Functions</strong> dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927947 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01328: Working with Security Structure diagram

- page_id: `191927947`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927947/Working+with+Security+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.268+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Security Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Security Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Security Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Resource Mitigation element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Resource Mitigation in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Mitigation and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Resource Mitigation in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Security Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Mitigation and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_resource_mitigation.png']

Resource Mitigation

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Mitigation (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Resource Mitigation

When you have Resource Mitigation created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and when it is highlighted in blue, click to finish the action.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Composition** and choose **Directed** **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='security_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. 
  1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
  2. Do one of the following:
    - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
    - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
  3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
  4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79f2566e-c3e5-47b2-bff2-a6c2cc613d24" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Security Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Security Structure and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Security Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="210" ac:width="319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_security_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Security Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Resource Mitigation element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Resource Mitigation in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Resource Mitigation and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="285" ac:width="481"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_security_resource_mitigation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Resource Mitigation in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Security Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Resource Mitigation and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="551" ac:width="353"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_security_resource_mitigation_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="217" ac:width="771"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_resource_mitigation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Mitigation</span> can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Internal Connectivity (Resources) diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Mitigation</span> (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Mitigation</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Resource Mitigation created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_composition_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and when it is highlighted in blue, click to finish the action.<br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="324" ac:width="442"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Directed Composition</strong> and choose <strong>Directed</strong> <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="117" ac:width="356"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="192" ac:width="407"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="665" ac:width="405"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6092e237-8611-4022-aa86-a673a74cbb51"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="857" ac:width="574"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b79737c-2b79-40d4-b56c-eef1be9713e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="724" ac:width="715"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927964 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01329: Working with Security Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927964`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927964/Working+with+Security+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:00.314+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1159118804

1159118806

1159118803

**On this page**

1159118805

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Security Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Security Enclave element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_enclave.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Security Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927973 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01330: Working with Security Taxonomy table

- page_id: `191927973`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927973/Working+with+Security+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T09:02:04.890+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Capability Configuration, Data Element, Information Element, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Asset dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>To create a Security Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Capability Configuration, Data Element, Information Element, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Asset</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927664 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01331: Working with Service Sequences diagram

- page_id: `191927664`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927664/Working+with+Service+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:11:05.440+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Service Exchange in the Service Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized****Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Service Roles, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and on the diagram palette select the Service Message.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized </strong><strong>Service Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange_palette.png"><ri:page Message.</span></li></ul></li><li>Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927612 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01332: Working with Services Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927612`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927612/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.833+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Services Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Services Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_connectivity_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Services Connectivity and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service element, but the same is valid for other Service specialization elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Service Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_service_to_diagram.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or other appropriate elements).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

A Services Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Services, Physical Location requirements, Operational Performers.

The structure of each Service can be modeled using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

##### Creating Service Exchanges

Before creating Service Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Service Signal, Resource Performer). Then you can start creating Service Exchanges.

To create a Service Exchange in the Service Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select a Service and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Service Exchange button. Click another Service to connect. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_exchange_smart_manipulator.png']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Service Exchange**and on the diagram pane, connect Services. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Service Exchanges on Associations

When you have Services connected with an Association, there are two ways to create service exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create a new Service Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Services.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Service Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_exchange_on_association.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_realized_on_association.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open a **Service Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_manager_from_smart_manipulator.png']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Service Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Service Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f86e50b5-ba39-4ae3-937e-031aacdf2476" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p>To create an Services Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Services Connectivity and do one of the following:</span><ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Services Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="258" ac:width="320"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_connectivity_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Services Connectivity </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. Search for the Service and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="404" ac:width="350"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="289" ac:width="848"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_service_to_diagram.png"><ri:page diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>In the wizard, specify the needed information. </p><p>In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927637 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01333: Working with Services Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `191927637`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927637/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-06T13:11:00.157+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Services Connectivity Matrix (Sv-Cn) show how services interact with each other. It is an editable matrix where cells represent Service Exchanges and the headers represent Services.

To create a Services Connectivity Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Services);
2. Specify Column Scope (Services);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

To add new Service Exchange

1. Double-click (or right-click) an empty intersection. The Add New Service Exchange button appears.
2. Click the Add New Service Exchange button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish .

To remove the exchange

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Services Connectivity Matrix (Sv-Cn) show how services interact with each other. It is an editable matrix where cells represent Service Exchanges and  the headers represent Services.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Services Connectivity Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add new Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click (or right-click) an empty intersection. The <strong>Add New Service Exchange</strong> button appears.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add New Service Exchange</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927633 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01334: Working with Services Connectivity table

- page_id: `191927633`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927633/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.910+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**The Services Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)** addresses service exchanges between services.

Service Exchanges in this table

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the Services Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Service Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the Service Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Service Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR2']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Service Connectivity Table report

## PAGE 01335: Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `191927643`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- version_date: `2022-09-16T11:47:42.877+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Services Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Service.
2. Connect Service using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The Services Constraints Definition diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Services Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service.</li><li>Connect Service using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p>The Services Constraints Definition diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service mapping.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927641 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01336: Working with Services Constraints table

- page_id: `191927641`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927641/Working+with+Services+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-06-07T12:51:40.738+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Service Constraints** (Sv-Ct) table specifies constraints applying to the implementation of Services, as well as constraints governing the use of one or more Services.

To create a Services Constraints table by adding a new Service Policy or Service Contract

1. Click the Add New button and choose either Service Policy or Service Contract. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and
  1. for the Service Policy, in the Select Service dialog, select constrained Services .
  2. for the Service Contract, in the **Select Operational Connector** dialog, select Operational Connector.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification**cell,click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).
5. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

Additional constrained elements can be added and edited for every row in the table.

To add an existing Service Policy or Service Contract

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Elements dialog, select Service Policies or Service Contract. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927625 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01337: Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927625`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927625/Working+with+Services+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T08:10:31.347+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Services Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Services Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Service Roles .
2. Associate Service Roles using Service Connectors.
3. Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.

##### Creating Service Exchange in Service Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Service Exchangein the Service Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange.png'] and then click the Service Connector.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Service Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Service Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Service Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram with Service Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01338: Working with Services Parametric diagram

- page_id: `191927645`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927645/Working+with+Services+Parametric+diagram

496918799

496918802

496918800

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of other Services. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Service Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Service context. All properties displayed other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496918797

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9966dff3-ee42-4014-a5f8-8fb8b0c7be29"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496918799</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fe22c36-cef8-40e8-b782-84df1e8deb6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496918802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85dd486a-4e55-4bf2-8595-75c42e897f44"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496918800</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> to constrain the properties of other Services. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Service Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Service context. All properties displayed other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfb492fb-c4ee-4dfd-a9cd-6e576fc5b528"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496918797</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927651 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01340: Working with Services Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `191927651`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927651/Working+with+Services+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.061+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] must be created first.

To create a Service Process Flow diagram for Service Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Service or Service Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel** in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Service or Service Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Service Function Action or drop Service Function from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Service Function Action wit h Service Control Flow or Service Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Service Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ServiceProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Service Function Action or Service Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Service Process Flow diagram for a Service Function Action or Service Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a ServiceProcess Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Service Function Action or Service Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Service Function Action or Service Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Service Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Service or Service Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new ServiceProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Service Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Service and Service Function (the behavior of the Service Function Action).

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR2']

## PAGE 01341: Working with Services Roadmap diagram

- page_id: `191927655`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927655/Working+with+Services+Roadmap+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-12T14:12:55.204+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

To define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and its specializationsand drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service and its specializationsfrom a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service and its specializations

1. Right-click the Service and its specializations bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the Service and its specializations you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
