<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927635 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01342: Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table

- page_id: `191927635`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927635/Working+with+Services+Role-based+Connectivity+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.975+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The Services Role-based Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)** specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between services.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR2']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To <span>create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use</span> Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Service Functions</h3><p>To add Service Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Service Functions</strong> button. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Producing and Consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[choose the producing and consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_role_based_connectivity_table_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report</span></h6><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927672 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01343: Working with Services Structure diagram

- page_id: `191927672`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927672/Working+with+Services+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.176+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a Services Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Services Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Services Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Services Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_service_specification.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Services

When Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='services_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927688 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01344: Working with Services Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927688`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927688/Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:59.227+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1160452963

1160452966

1160452961

**On this page**

1160452965

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Services Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Services Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_services_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Services Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service Specification element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_specification.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Services Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service Specification (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Specification (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7bf3535-e44f-451e-b2d1-0cc8a1c2270b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="464f2ced-5f48-4f87-ac0a-5a6c3aebaec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452966</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c7201c0-f59d-40bc-84e1-3f12670ab88b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452961</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e8e0af5-f0bb-4b00-b75e-1b22bb933699" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adfd81ac-5288-4cb6-8c91-56c59d94216d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Services Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Services Taxonomy and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Services Taxonomy</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="281" ac:width="313"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_services_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Services Taxonomy </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service Specification element, but the same is valid for other elements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="259" ac:width="506"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an element in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Services Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Search for the Service Specification </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or other appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="486" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_specification_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="220" ac:width="751"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_service_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Service Specification (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Service Specification (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_pallet_services.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.</span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="257" ac:width="988"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_smart_services.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make an element abstract</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927697 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01345: Working with Services Taxonomy table

- page_id: `191927697`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927697/Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-16T11:57:22.098+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Services Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button, and in the Select Service dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Services Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button, and in the <strong>Select Service </strong>dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927702 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01346: Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927702`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927702/Working+with+Services+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-28T13:12:31.266+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282964251

1282964262

1282964261

The Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enable.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create a Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope ( Service ).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Serviceexposes Capabilities using the Exhibitsrelationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282964250

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927704 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01347: Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927704`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927704/Working+with+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-28T13:17:09.792+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282974676

1282974687

1282974677

The Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Servicesand the Columns are Operational Activities.

To create a Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Operational Activities);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282974666

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927706 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01348: Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927706`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927706/Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-10-21T08:43:48.262+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927259 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01349: Working with Sr Services Roadmap

- page_id: `191927259`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927259/Working+with+Sr+Services+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-16T11:45:57.542+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > Sr Service Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921716523

INLINE

921716525

INLINE

921716524

INLINE

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service from a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service

1. Right-click the Service bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

ac:ref="0f1b80b5-4323-4636-a93f-e4022ad83ee4">Do one of the following:</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span></span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Service</strong>.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the diagram pane, right-click a <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Version Of Configuration</span></span> and select <strong>Service</strong>. </span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Containment tree, select a Service and drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> opens in the second step.)<br /></span></span></span>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The result of this procedure is as the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-8_version_of_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Sr Services Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Removing Service from a Services Roadmap diagram</h3><p>To remove a Service</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Service bar, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Remove </strong>&gt;<strong> Service </strong>&lt;name of the resource you want to remove&gt;.</li></ol><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram</h3><p>To delete a Version of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Version of Configuration.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928045 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01350: Working with Standards Roadmap table

- page_id: `191928045`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928045/Working+with+Standards+Roadmap+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-05T12:11:02.602+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards > Standards Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658889191

1658889203

1658889192

To create a Standards Roadmap table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658889189

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928049 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01351: Working with Standards Structure diagram

- page_id: `191928049`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928049/Working+with+Standards+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.090+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards > Standards Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1233356123

1233356126

1233356121

**On this page**

1233356125

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Standards Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Standards Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Standards Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Standards Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Protocol element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Protocol in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Protocol and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Protocol in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Standards Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Protocol and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_protocol.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Protocol (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Protocols

When you have Protocol created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Protocol Layers (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.change_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards_comparments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

1233362204

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024xR2']

ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Protocol Layers (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="281" ac:width="352"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="201" ac:width="315"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="315"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.change_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8531680-d4c7-4ff0-ac31-c8bbb22dad98"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="856" ac:width="501"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_comparments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47006c6c-2346-4e29-a79c-0528261134c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="761" ac:width="696"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e296adc4-a21b-4a67-8ced-d13a01ff9aa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1233362204</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928065 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01352: Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191928065`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928065/Working+with+Standards+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.156+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards > Standards Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1424709417

1424709419

1424709416

**On this page**

1424709418

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Standards Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Standards Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Standards Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_standards_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Standards Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Standard element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Standard (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_standard.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Standards Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Standard (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Standard (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Standard (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Generalization.
3. Connect the Standard elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928075 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01353: Working with Standards Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `191928075`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928075/Working+with+Standards+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-06-20T13:16:30.251+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards > Standards Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Standards Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button to add a Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Standard dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to add a Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928078 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01354: Working with Standards Traceability table

- page_id: `191928078`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928078/Working+with+Standards+Traceability+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-06T10:11:50.136+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards > Standards Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Standards Traceability table

- Create a new element: 1) Click the Add New button. 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element. 3) In the Select Owner dialog choose the owner and click OK . 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click Close . The element appears under the Resource element column. 5) Select the cell under the Standard/Policy column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 6) In the Select Standard dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.
- Add an existing element. 
 1) Click the**Add Existing** button. 
 2) In the **Select UAF element** dialog select desired elements and click **OK**. The element appears under the **Resource element** column. 
 3) Select the cell under the **Standard/Policy** column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 4) In the **Select Standard** dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Standards Traceability table</p><hr /><ul><li>Create a new element:<br /> 1) Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.<br /> 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element.<br /> 3) In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog choose the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click <strong>Close</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>Resource element</strong> column.<br /> 5) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Traceability table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> .<br /> 6) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.<span> </span></li><li><p>Add an existing element.<br /> 1) Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button.<br /> 2) In the <strong>Select UAF element</strong> dialog select desired elements and click <strong>OK</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>Resource element</strong> column.<br /> 3) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Traceability table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> .<br /> 4) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.</p></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927431 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01355: Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram

- page_id: `191927431`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927431/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-05T12:07:16.434+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923113496

923113498

923113495

**On this page**

923113497

##### Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table

The context of the Strategic Actual Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Structure' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the Strategic Actual Deployment table is Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Actual Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

###### emptyCreate a StrategicActual Deployment table

To create an empty Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > Strategic Actual Deployment .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > Strategic Actual Deployment .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Phases relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-**button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Actual Deployment table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

228689575

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927478 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01356: Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `191927478`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927478/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Strategic+Phase+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-09-20T10:24:20.646+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The** **Strategic Actual **Strategic** Phase Taxonomy Table (St-Tx)** specifies all the Actual Strategic Phases referenced throughout one or more architectures. The view can also be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

To add a new Actual Strategic Phase

- Click the Add New button and choose one of the following:
  - Actual Enduring Task
  - Actual Enterprise Phase
  - Enterprise Mission
  - Value Stream
  - Whole Life Enterprise

To add an existing Actual Strategic Phase

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, select the existing Actual Strategic Phase.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The</strong> <strong>Strategic Actual <strong>Strategic</strong> Phase Taxonomy Table (St-Tx)</strong> specifies all the Actual Strategic Phases referenced throughout one or more architectures. The view can also be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements. </p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Actual Strategic Phase </p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and choose one of the following:<ul><li>Actual Enduring Task</li><li>Actual Enterprise Phase</li><li>Enterprise Mission</li><li>Value Stream</li><li>Whole Life Enterprise</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To add an existing <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Strategic Phase</span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog, select the existing <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Strategic Phase.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="">You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul style=""><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927404 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01357: Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `191927404`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927404/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_connectivity_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic **Connectivity** and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Actual Enterprise Phase element, but the same is valid for other Actual Strategic Phase elements.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Actual Enterprise Phase and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_palette.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Actual Enterprise Phase and select it. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_containment_tree.png']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_capability_to_diagram.png']

The structure of each Capability can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose an appropriate element.

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [Creating elements from other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Creating+elements+from+other+resources).

##### Creating Strategic Exchanges

Before creating Strategic Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Resource Performer, Strategic Information, Geo Politic Extent Type or their specializations). The you can start creating Strategic Exchanges.

To create a Strategic Exchange in the Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Actual Enterprise Phase and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Strategic Exchange. Click another Actual Enterprise Phase to connect. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_sm.png']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Strategic Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_pallete.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Strategic Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Strategic Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Strategic Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Strategic Exchange**wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927413 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01358: Working with Strategic Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `191927413`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927413/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.820+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283082281

1283082283

1283082282

The Strategic Connectivity Matrix (St-Cn) shows the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']) dependencies between the Capabilities.

The Rows and the Columns of this matrix are Capabilities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

Capabilities map to Capabilities using Dependency relationship.

To map Capability to Capability

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1283082280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
