<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927417 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01359: Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `191927417`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927417/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.883+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_strategic_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_capability_for_strategic_constraints.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Strategic Constraints package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating and applying Strategic Constraints

You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.

To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed Capability a nd open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Strategic > Strategic Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.

To apply Strategic Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability .
- In the needed Capability Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the <strong>Apply </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> window, choose a constraint.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927422 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01360: Working with Strategic Constraints table

- page_id: `191927422`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927422/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-08-11T13:41:37.452+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Strategic Constraints**table specifies constraints that apply to capabilities.

To create a Strategic Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the**Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint**dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Strategic Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Strategic Constraints</strong><span> </span>table specifies constraints that apply to capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Constraints table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button. The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in the<span> </span><strong>Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint</strong><span> </span>dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Strategic Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table. </li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Strategic Constraints table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol>
````
